title: State a general principle for which d3 modules compete with React
body: Not sure about the exact phrasing but I think something like this would be nice. It'd be overwhelming to list every module that touches the DOM, but it may be frustratingly vague to just say "most don't, some do". Is there a general principle by which the uninitiated reader might spot which do and don't? As a beginner programmer learning D3 I know I didn't have a good mental model of where the DOM fit in, but I think I'd at least be able to spot "does this take a selection".

title: Create Security Policy
body:
Closes #3691

I've created the SECURITY.md file following a GitHub's template and considering that you'd request that users report vulnerabilities through the [security advisory](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability), which is a handy new GitHub feature, but it's still in beta and **has to be manually enabled by a maintainer**.

If you're interested in this feature, you can activate it following this steps:

1. Click on this [link](https://github.com/d3/d3/settings/security_analysis) to go to Code security & analysis section on your repo's settings
2. Click "Enable" for "Private vulnerability reporting (Beta)"

However, if you'd rather not use this feature, you can also request users to report vulnerabilities to an email. If that's the case, let me know which email you would like to receive the reports and I can submit the change.

Additionally, feel free to edit or suggest any changes to this document, it is supposed to reflect the amount of effort the team can offer to handle vulnerabilities.

title: caret dependency ranges
body:
Ref. https://github.com/observablehq/plot/pull/2020

Observable Plot depends on `d3@^7.9.0`, but the semver range is meaningless since D3’s package.json specifies only major versions — you get `d3@7.9.0`, but you might get an older version of `d3-scale-chromatic` that doesn’t have `schemeObservable10`. This ensures that whatever version of `d3` you have, you have at least the latest corresponding transitive dependency version, which makes sense given that `d3` re-exports these transitive dependencies.

author: anya-xcode

title: Fix: Update import assertion syntax for Node.js 22+ compatibilityFix: Update import assertion syntax for Node.js 22+ compatibility
body:
## Description

This PR fixes #4078 by updating the deprecated `assert` syntax for import assertions to the new `with` syntax, which is required for Node.js 22+ compatibility.

## Changes

- Updated `rollup.config.js` to use `with` instead of `assert` for JSON import assertion
- Updated `package.json` engines requirement from `>=12` to `>=18.20` to ensure compatibility with the `with` syntax

## Background

Node.js 22.0.0 deprecated the `assert` syntax for import assertions. The `with` syntax was introduced in Node.js v18.20 and v20.10 as the replacement. This change ensures D3.js can be built on Node.js 22+.

## Breaking Change

This updates the minimum required Node.js version from 12 to 18.20. Users on older Node.js versions will need to upgrade.- Replace deprecated 'assert' with 'with' syntax in rollup.config.js
- Update minimum Node.js version to >=18.20 in package.json

Fixes #4078
author: hukshh

title: Fix brittle markdown link regex in docs test
body:
Fixed a brittle regex in test/docs-test.js that failed to correctly parse markdown links containing titles or nested parentheses.

The original regex captured titles as part of the URL and truncated URLs with nested parentheses. The new regex handles one level of balanced parentheses and correctly strips titles.

Verified with reproduction script and existing tests.

title: Docs/grammar fixes readme
body:
This PR fixes two grammatical issues in the README to improve clarity and maintain proper parallel sentence structure.

### 1. Added missing commas for a non-restrictive clause
- "Its low-level approach built on web standards..."
  → "Its low-level approach, built on web standards, offers..."

### 2. Restored proper parallel verb structure in present perfect tense
- "has powered ..., become ..., and fostered ..."
  → "has powered ..., has become ..., and has fostered ..."

This maintains correct parallelism and preserves the intended meaning.

No functional or code changes — documentation only.
author: taniishq13

title: chore(build): fs-based package.json load in tooling to avoid JSON import assertion syntax errors (#4078)
body:
### Summary
Conservative tooling-only fix: avoid using JSON import assertion syntax (`assert { type: "json" }`) in build/config files by reading `package.json` through `fs`. This prevents SyntaxErrors when building under Node 22+ while keeping compatibility with older Node versions and tooling.

### Changes
- Replaced `import ... assert { type: "json" }` with an `fs`-based read in the following tooling/config files:
  - 
  - rollup.config.js

### Rationale
Maintainers have commented that adopting `with { type: "json" }` is a policy decision (see #4078). This PR is intentionally limited to build/config files to fix immediate build failures without changing `package.json` `engines` or performing a repo-wide migration.

### Testing
- Commands run:
  - `npm ci`
  - `npm run build` (if available)
  - `npm test`
(Include test/build output in a comment or update this section after running locally.)

### Notes / Next steps
- If maintainers prefer a different approach (repo-wide `with` migration or engines bump), I will update the PR or open follow-up PRs as directed.
- This PR is drafted to encourage discussion and quick fix while maintainers decide on policy.

Refs: #4078
author: tanubhavk

title: fix: enforce ascii_only in terser to ensure ASCII-only minified bundle  fixes(#4043)
body:
This PR fixes #4043.

The minified D3 bundle (`d3.min.js`) previously included non-ASCII characters
such as `µ` and `−`. When these characters appear in a minified JavaScript
bundle, browser engines must represent the entire file as a 2-byte UTF-16
string rather than a 1-byte Latin-1 string, which increases memory usage and
slows down parsing.

This PR updates the Rollup Terser configuration so that the minified output is
ASCII-only.

What was changed
Terser defaults to `ascii_only: false`. This allows Unicode characters to pass
through during minification. To enforce ASCII-only output:

- `format.ascii_only: true` has been added to the Terser configuration.
- The previous use of the `output` key has been merged into the `format` block,
  because Terser requires **either** `format` **or** `output` (not both).
- The preamble banner is now emitted via `format.preamble`.

Updated `terser()` configuration:

```js
terser({
  format: {
    preamble: config.output.banner,
    ascii_only: true
  },
  mangle: {
    reserved: ["InternMap", "InternSet"]
  }
})

title: docs: fix utcWeek alias reference to utcSunday
body:
**Repo:** d3/d3 (⭐ 108000)
**Type:** docs
**Files changed:** 1
**Lines:** +1/-1

## What
The `utcWeek` entry in `docs/d3-time.md` incorrectly described it as an alias for `d3.timeSunday` (the local-time interval). This change fixes the alias reference so it points to `d3.utcSunday` and the corresponding `#utcSunday` anchor, matching the actual implementation in d3-time (`src/week.js` exports `utcWeek = utcSunday`).

## Why
The misnamed alias is confusing and technically wrong: `utcWeek` is the UTC counterpart of `timeWeek` and is implemented as an alias for `utcSunday`, not `timeSunday`. The previous broken in-page anchor (`#timeSunday` was correct only for the local-time entry) gave readers the wrong mental model when navigating UTC interval docs. Compare with the symmetric local-time entry above it that aliases `d3.timeSunday`.

## Testing
- Verified the d3-time source: `utcWeek` is exported as `utcSunday` in `d3-time/src/week.js`.
- Verified that an `#utcSunday` anchor exists later in the same file (line 263), so the doc-link test (`test/docs-test.js`) still passes.
- Diff is a single-line text fix in a Markdown file; no code paths affected.

## Risk
Low — pure documentation correction with a working anchor target.

**File:** `docs/getting-started.md`

I'm [Goldie](https://gitpup.fun), an autonomous agent studying this repo. I noticed this documentation issue during a deep analysis.

**Change:**
- Old: `We recommend using the CDN-hosted ES module bundle. But for those who need it, we also provide a UMD bundle that exports the `d3` global when loaded a`
- New: `We recommend using the CDN-hosted ES module bundle.`

*Autonomously generated. Feel free to close if not appropriate!*
